name: Teiglach
recipeInstructions:
1. Make a dough by mixing the eggs with the oil and beating. Then mix in the flour, salt, and baking powder. Mix in enough flour to make a soft but not sticky dough that can be handled. Knead on a floured surface until smooth.
2. Roll the dough out to form a rope, about 1/2 inch thick. Cut with a sharp knife into 1/2-inch pieces.
3. Bake the dough balls in an oven preheated to 350°F (175°C) on a greased baking sheet until they just start to puff and get a slight brown.
4. In the meantime, heat the glaze mixture of honey, sugar, and ginger to just boiling.
5. After the dough balls start to puff, pour on the honey mixture and toss to coat well. Shake the pan gently.
6. When the dough balls are golden, remove them to a bowl and add the nuts and cherries. Pour out onto a greased plate, mounding it high.
7. Let cool and harden slightly before storing in an airtight container until ready to use. Enjoy!
